title: STANDARD FOR PICKLED FRUITS AND VEGETABLES

sections.section: STANDARD FOR PICKLED FRUITS AND VEGETABLES
sections.summary: Title and identification of the Codex standard: 'Standard for Pickled Fruits and Vegetables' CXS 260-2007, adopted 2007 and amended in 2015, 2017, 2022 and 2023.
Notes that 2022 amendments, following the 45th Codex Alimentarius Commission, revised Section 8.3 on labelling of non-retail containers.
Notes that 2023 amendments, following the 46th Codex Alimentarius Commission, amended the wording of Section 9 on methods of analysis and sampling.

Applies the standard to pickled fruits and vegetables offered for direct consumption (including catering and repacking) — lists covered commodities such as onions, garlic, mango, ginger, etc. — excludes products intended for further processing and excludes pickled cucumbers, kimchi, table olives, sauerkraut, chutneys and relishes.
Introduces the product description section covering the product definition, permitted styles and types of pack for pickled fruits and vegetables (details provided in subsections 2.1–2.3).

Defines pickled fruits and vegetables as products made from sound, clean, edible fruits and/or vegetables (with optional seeds, spices, herbs or condiments), preserved by natural fermentation or added acidulants, processed and hermetically sealed as appropriate to ensure safety and prevent spoilage, and packed with or without a suitable liquid packing medium so that the equilibrium pH is less than 4.6.
Allows any presentation style (e.g., whole, pieces, halves, quarters, cubes, shredded or chopped) provided the product meets all requirements of the standard.
Defines two pack types: solid pack (without added packing medium) and regular pack (with a packing medium as specified in Section 3.1.2).

Heading introducing essential composition and quality factors for pickled fruits and vegetables, and indicating the standard addresses composition, packing media, quality criteria, defect definitions, classification of defectives and lot acceptance.

Defines composition for pickled fruits and vegetables: basic ingredients are fruits/vegetables with an appropriate liquid packing medium, packing media rules for fruits and vegetables, and a list of other permitted ingredients in Section 3.1.3.
Specifies that basic ingredients are fruits and vegetables with a liquid packing medium when appropriate, combined with one or more other permitted ingredients listed in Section 3.1.3.

Sets packing media rules: pickled fruits follow CXG 51-2003; pickled vegetables' basic media are water and, if necessary, salt, oil or acidic media, and optional ingredients (subject to Section 8 labelling) may include sweetening foods, aromatics/spices, vinegar, vegetable oils, tomato puree, malt extract, sauces (e.g. fish), soy sauce and other appropriate ingredients.

Lists other permitted ingredients that may be used, including cereal grains, dried fruits, malt extract, nuts, pulses, sauces (e.g. fish), soy sauce, sweetening foods such as sugars and honey, and other appropriate ingredients.

Requires the product to have characteristic colour, flavour, odour and texture and sets additional quality provisions including minimum oil content where applicable, adequate salt/acidity for preservation, definitions of defects and allowances.

Specifies further quality criteria: minimum oil percentage for products in oil, sufficient salt or acidity for brined/acidified products, definitions of blemishes and harmless extraneous material, and that products should be practically free from such defects.

sections.section: 3.2.1.1 Pickled fruits and/or vegetables in edible oil
sections.summary: For pickled fruits and/or vegetables in edible oil, the oil content shall be not less than ten percent by weight.

sections.section: 3.2.1.2 Pickled fruits and/or vegetables in brine or an acidic medium
sections.summary: For pickled fruits and/or vegetables in brine or an acidic medium, the salt concentration of the covering liquid or the media's acidity must be sufficient to ensure keeping quality and proper preservation.

Defines defects: 'blemishes' include characteristics such as bruises, scabs and dark discolouration that adversely affect appearance, and 'harmless extraneous material' means non‑hazardous vegetable parts (e.g. leaves, stems) that affect appearance.
States that the product should be practically free from defects as defined in Section 3.2.
Declares a container that fails to meet one or more applicable quality requirements in Section 3.2 (except those based on sample averages) should be considered a 'defective'.

Specifies lot acceptance: a lot meets applicable quality requirements when non‑average requirements have defectives not exceeding the sampling plan's acceptance number (c) at an AQL of 6.5, and requirements based on sample averages are complied with.

Permits specified classes of food additives (acidity regulators, antifoaming agents, antioxidants, colours, colour retention agents, firming agents, flavour enhancers, preservatives, sequestrants, stabilizers and sweeteners) when used in accordance with Tables 1–2 (and Table 3 where applicable) of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192‑1995) for the relevant food category.
Requires compliance with the maximum levels in the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193‑1995) and with Codex‑established maximum residue limits for pesticides.

Recommends preparing and handling products in accordance with the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1‑1969), the Code of Hygienic Practice for Low and Acidified Low‑Acid Canned Foods (CXC 23‑1979) and other relevant Codex texts, and states products should comply with any microbiological criteria established under CXG 21‑1997.

Addresses weights and measures including fill of container, minimum drained weight percentages by style, defective classification for underfilled containers, and lot acceptance rules (including sampling and average‑based compliance).

Requires containers be filled to at least 90% of their water capacity (minus necessary headspace), treats underfilled containers as defectives with lot acceptance under a sampling plan at AQL 6.5, and specifies minimum drained weight percentages for different product styles.

Mandates that the sealed container be well filled so the product (including packing medium) occupies not less than 90% of the container's water capacity (minus necessary head space), where water capacity is the volume of distilled water at 20°C the sealed container holds when completely filled.
States that any container failing to meet the minimum fill requirement of Section 7.1.1 shall be considered a 'defective'.
Specifies lot acceptance: a lot meets Section 7.1.1 if the number of defectives (per Section 7.1.2) does not exceed the acceptance number (c) of the sampling plan with an AQL of 6.5.
